summary: Epic Dark Star, blistering Blow Away, tight jams across both sets
blurb: The show delivers a monster 26‑minute Dark Star and a scorching Blow Away in the second set. Brent’s keyboards shine on Estimated Prophet and The Wheel while the rhythm section drives an intense Drum/Space segment. The first set opens strong with Foolish Heart and stays tight through blues‑rock cuts.
review: October 26, 1989 at Miami Arena finds the Dead deep in a dark‑era groove that still feels fresh today. A flawless opening with Foolish Heart rolls into a blues‑heavy first set – Little Red Rooster, Stagger Lee, Brown Eyed Women and Victim Or The Crime all land solidly, but it’s the second set that cements this night’s legend. An otherworldly Estimated Prophet segues straight into a blistering Blow Away before launching into an expansive Dark Star that stretches past 26 minutes, morphing through a powerful Drum/Space excursion and surfacing into The Wheel, All Along The Watchtower, Stella Blue and Not Fade Away. Brent Mydland’s keyboards are on fire – lush harmonies on Estimated Prophet, manic piano on Blow Away and shimmering synth textures throughout the jam. Jerry’s voice may be “froggy” but his guitar is nothing short of a monster, especially during Dark Star and the closing Not Fade Away. Phil’s low‑frequency bass underpins the whole thing, giving the improvisations a dense, grounding current, while Bill and Mickey drive the percussion to a hot, crowd‑piercing climax.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across all recordings the sentiment is overwhelmingly positive – multiple reviewers call the Dark Star "epic", "monster" and "otherworldly", praise Blow Away as "blistering" or "stellar", and highlight Brent's keyboards as a "keyboard god". Only a few recordings note mixed audio quirks, but none mention consistent performance flaws. The consensus of strong musical moments yields a high positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crisp, vivid stereo imaging and no audible drop‑outs; captures the full power of Dark Star and Blow Away.

key_highlights: Monster 26‑minute Dark Star that dominates the second set
Blazing Blow Away right after Estimated Prophet
Intense Drum/Space jam driving the psychedelic climax
Brent’s lush keyboards adding depth to The Wheel and Estimated Prophet

band_performance.Jerry: His voice is froggy yet powerful, and his guitar tone on Dark Star and Not Fade Away is described as unreal and monster‑like; he drives the dark vibe with intense solos.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a dense, low‑frequency bass foundation that adds an undercurrent to the jams; praised as a "monster" on Brown Eyed Women and generally solid throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Adds eerie whale‑like textures on Stella Blue and Watchtower, contributing vocal grit and occasional spooky embellishments.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is a highlight – called a "keyboard god", delivering lush harmonies on Estimated Prophet, manic piano on Blow Away, and rich synth layers throughout the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers powerful percussion, especially during the extended Drum segment that drives the space jam to a hot climax.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a tight, driving groove; his drumming shines on Victim Or The Crime and powers the intense Drum/Space excursion.
